meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve Debugging
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master debugging. A high-impact, intermediate-level checklist for student developers.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis stems from poor architectural visibility. By integrating the Antigravity project template, you establish a standardized, observable codebase. Pair this with Claude Code to automate trace analysis and iterative refactoring, transforming your build process from a chaotic trial-and-error cycle into a predictable, professional engineering workflow.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of red text, the cursor blinking in mocking rhythm. Your code, once a vision of innovation, has collapsed into a labyrinth of 'undefined' errors and silent state failures. The frustration isn't just about the bug—it's the feeling that you’ve hit a ceiling where your ambition outpaces your technical reach.

But the gap between a student and a senior engineer isn't raw intelligence; it’s the tooling stack. The Antigravity project template provides the structural scaffolding that prevents these collapses before they start, while Claude Code acts as your infinite-memory pair programmer. It is time to stop fighting your editor and start engineering with precision.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity Scaffolding
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure observability. This prevents 'spaghetti state' that makes debugging impossible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity template, ensuring strict TypeScript types, modular folder architecture, and integrated error logging middleware.

steps.title: Automate Traceability with Claude Code
steps.description: Configure Claude Code to monitor your build logs. This creates a real-time feedback loop between your code changes and runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project observability layer to output structured logs that Claude Code can parse during runtime errors.

steps.title: Perform Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Stop guessing. Use Claude Code to analyze the stack trace against your Antigravity architecture to isolate the failure point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current stack trace and cross-reference it with the Antigravity project configuration to identify the specific module causing the memory leak.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze stack trace and map to component'

steps.title: Apply Declarative Fixes
steps.description: Instead of manual patching, instruct the AI to refactor the module using the template's design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified module to adhere to the Antigravity 'service-provider' pattern, ensuring state consistency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'refactor module to service-provider pattern'

steps.title: Validate with Automated Regression
steps.description: Ensure the fix holds up under stress by running a suite of integrated tests generated by the template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of regression tests for the fixed module using the Antigravity testing framework.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate and execute regression tests'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'debug-log' stream that feeds directly into your AI context window for instant correlation.
Treat your Antigravity template as a living contract; if you deviate from the folder structure, update the documentation immediately.
Use Claude Code's '--context' flag to inject your PRD into the terminal session so the AI understands your business logic, not just the syntax.
When a bug persists, ask the AI to 'explain the state flow' rather than asking it to 'fix the code' to build your own mental model.
Automate your documentation updates as part of the fix cycle to ensure your codebase never drifts from its original architecture.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is stable, move to implementing CI/CD pipelines that trigger your Claude Code analysis on every pull request.
content.conversion_block: Stop hacking together solutions and start building architecture that scales. Your portfolio deserves the polish of a production-grade app. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
